key_insight: Catalyst discovery for ammonia synthesis is reframed as a multi-intermediate reaction-network problem, and ten-million-scale generative search exposes rare surfaces compatible with all key intermediates.
engineering_relevance: This approach can accelerate catalyst screening by identifying materials that jointly satisfy competing reaction-pathway constraints, rather than optimizing a single descriptor.
method: Adsorbate-specific generative Transformers explore ~15 million configurations per intermediate (N*, NH*, NNH*, HNNH*).
Structural compression plus machine-learning-potential screening maps compatibility across the four-intermediate reaction network.
Representative DFT validation confirms pathway-specific candidates for dissociative and associative mechanisms.
evidence_signal: The full four-intermediate compatibility space was only discovered at ten-million-scale exploration, yielding 279 high-potential target materials and recovering known Fe/Ru motifs alongside new families.
watch_item: ML-potential screening may miss subtle DFT-level effects, so the final candidate set still needs careful first-principles validation before experimental prioritization.
paper_title: Reaction-Network-Level Discovery of Ammonia Synthesis Catalysts via Ten-Million-Scale Generative Exploration
